name: Claude Opus 4.8
verdict: Instantly readable: a proper walrus with both tusks and whiskers, an upright cello with a scroll, strings and an endpin. The f-holes went missing, and the bow — suspiciously walrus-colored — seems to have fused with the flipper holding it. Roughly 5 of 7 on the checklist.

name: GPT-5.5
verdict: The most polished drawing of the batch — gradients, shading, tiny f-holes, a confident endpin. But the composition turns surreal: the cello is fused into the walrus's belly, the scroll floats above its head like a hair curl, the bow slices right through the tusks — and the walrus has no legs at all, the body just fades out below. Beautiful chaos; roughly 5 of 7.
